report_name: Gentlemen RaaS Expands with SystemBC Botnet for Covert Attacks

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: A ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group that emerged in mid-2025, providing multi-platform lockers for Windows, Linux, and ESXi. They leverage the SystemBC botnet to enhance attack sophistication and evasion, and operate a double-extortion model with a Tor-based leak site.

attack_patterns.name: Proxy
attack_patterns.description: The SystemBC botnet was used to establish covert SOCKS5 proxy tunnels on compromised hosts to obscure command-and-control traffic.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Command Shell
attack_patterns.description: Used by Gentlemen affiliates for executing commands and deploying tools like Cobalt Strike and SystemBC.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Gentlemen ransomware payload was detonated across victim networks to encrypt critical systems, including ESXi hosts, as the primary goal of the attack.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware targeted VMware ESXi hosts to inhibit system recovery by encrypting virtual machines and their snapshots, causing massive operational disruption.

malware.name: SystemBC
malware.description: A proxy malware and backdoor used by Gentlemen RaaS affiliates to create covert SOCKS5 tunnels on a botnet of compromised hosts, hiding C2 traffic and delivering ransomware payloads.

malware.name: Gentlemen Ransomware
malware.description: A multi-platform ransomware locker provided by The Gentlemen RaaS group. It includes a Go-based variant for Windows/Linux and a C-based version for VMware ESXi.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike
tools.description: Used by Gentlemen affiliates for command and control and network discovery after gaining initial access and escalating privileges.

infrastructure.name: SystemBC Botnet
infrastructure.description: A botnet of over 1,570 compromised corporate systems running SystemBC malware, used by Gentlemen affiliates to create SOCKS5 proxy tunnels for C2 traffic.

infrastructure.name: Gentlemen Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A Tor-based leak site used by The Gentlemen RaaS group for their double-extortion model, where they publish data from claimed victims.

indicators.name: SystemBC Masquerading as System Process
indicators.pattern:
title: SystemBC Masquerading as System Process
id: 2b1a9c8d-f3e4-4b5a-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects SystemBC malware masquerading as a legitimate system process such as system.exe or svchost.exe, often with an unusual parent process like explorer.exe or no parent.
references:
  - https://gbhackers.com/gentlemen-raas-hits-windows-linux-and-esxi/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\system.exe'
      - '\svchost.exe'
  filter_legit_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\services.exe'
      - '\rpcss.exe'
      - '\wininit.exe'
      - '\smss.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_parent
indicators.description: Detects SystemBC malware masquerading as a legitimate system process such as system.exe or svchost.exe, often with an unusual parent process like explorer.exe or no parent, as suggested in the report's hunting hints.

indicators.name: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution for Payload Delivery
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution for Payload Delivery
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4b-4c3d-2b1a-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of PowerShell with the '-enc' argument, a common technique for executing obfuscated commands, as seen in SystemBC deployment.
references:
  - https://gbhackers.com/gentlemen-raas-hits-windows-linux-and-esxi/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -EncodedCommand '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of PowerShell with the '-enc' argument to execute obfuscated commands, a technique noted for fileless download and execution of SystemBC.

indicators.name: Suspicious Executable Dropped in AppData Roaming
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Executable Dropped in AppData Roaming
id: 7c6b5a4b-3d2b-4a1f-9e8d-7c6b5a4b3d2b
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a randomly named executable file in the AppData\Roaming directory, a common location for malware like SystemBC to be dropped.
references:
  - https://gbhackers.com/gentlemen-raas-hits-windows-linux-and-esxi/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1564.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|startswith: 'C:\Users\'
    TargetFilename|contains: '\AppData\Roaming\'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of an executable file in the AppData\Roaming directory, a common location for malware like SystemBC to be dropped.

relationships.source_name: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution for Payload Delivery
Suspicious Executable Dropped in AppData Roaming
